jokes.text: "You bought a Bentley?" / "People... They'll follow you home and, and kill you." / "Yeah. Much safer in a Subaru Outback."
jokes.setup: Larry reveals he bought an expensive Bentley
jokes.punchline: Carl's paranoid prediction followed by Jeff's deadpan Subaru alternative
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David setup - shows his wealth creating awkward social dynamics, with good comedic escalation

jokes.text: "she's got this magical vagina" / "Huh? What the hell are you talking about?"
jokes.setup: Carl casually mentions his wife's supernatural anatomy
jokes.punchline: Larry and Jeff's shocked reactions to the TMI revelation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main running gag with perfect shock value and quotable phrase

jokes.text: Really? Cheryl and I had sex four times a week.
jokes.setup: Discussion of marital frequency
jokes.punchline: Larry oversharing about his sex life
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry oversharing TMI moment

jokes.text: "It's like this little hummingbird nest. It's got this mind of its own. It's just alive."
jokes.setup: Pressed for details about the magical vagina
jokes.punchline: Carl's bizarrely poetic and uncomfortable description
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd metaphor that makes the uncomfortable situation even more uncomfortable

jokes.text: Carl's violent Jets rant interrupting the vagina discussion
jokes.setup: Friends trying to calm Carl's sports obsession
jokes.punchline: Carl explosively telling them to shut up about his team
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carl's sports obsession that will lead to his downfall

jokes.text: The three of you, just eat your food and shut up. Leave me alone.
jokes.setup: Friends trying to console Carl about football
jokes.punchline: Carl's disproportionate anger over Jets disappointment
jokes.explanation: Shows Carl's sports-related emotional instability

jokes.text: "or is everyone in that section way better looking than the people in this section"
jokes.setup: Larry noticing restaurant seating patterns
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable realization about beauty-based segregation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David observation - turns social anxiety into comedy premise

jokes.text: "You look like Einstein's gardener, for Christ's sake."
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he should be in the attractive section
jokes.punchline: Jeff's brutal but specific insult about Larry's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted insult - specific, visual, and brutally accurate to Larry's appearance

jokes.text: "somebody at this table prevented us from sitting over there and I think you know who it is"
jokes.setup: Friends trying to figure out who's the ugly one
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable implication that one of them is dragging the group down
jokes.explanation: Good group dynamic comedy as friends turn on each other

jokes.text: You have a good-looking section and you have an ugly section. Why would we do that?
jokes.setup: Larry directly confronting the manager
jokes.punchline: Manager's flustered non-denial
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry confrontation with social awkwardness

jokes.text: Larry asking bathroom attendant to leave so he can defecate in privacy
jokes.setup: Larry needs to use bathroom with attendant present
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate attempts to get privacy
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social awkwardness - universal relatable situation made uncomfortable

jokes.text: This is not a job for human beings, to be here when other people are going to the bathroom. Nobody wants you here!
jokes.setup: Harold defending his bathroom attendant role
jokes.punchline: Larry's passionate argument against bathroom attendants
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social commentary with moral indignation

jokes.text: "When an animal goes to die, it leaves the pack. That's what needs to happen here. I need to be alone. I'm a dying animal."
jokes.setup: Larry trying to convince Harold to leave the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Comparing his bowel movement to a dying animal's final moments
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd metaphor that elevates bathroom humor through Larry's philosophical approach

jokes.text: I'm opening up a coffee shop, okay? Latte Larry's. I'll give you a free cup of coffee and a scone for a week.
jokes.setup: Larry negotiating with Harold
jokes.punchline: Shameless self-promotion even in bathroom negotiation
jokes.explanation: Larry's opportunistic business promotion in inappropriate context

jokes.text: "Most of the people who come in here are gonna be, you know, just disgusting human beings. Vile. Vulgar. The dregs of society."
jokes.setup: Larry interviewing Sam for coffee shop job
jokes.punchline: Larry describing his future customers in the most unflattering terms possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David misanthropy - brutal honesty about customer service reality

jokes.text: Sam diagnosing Larry's rash, Larry saying he'll see his father instead "'Cause he's a doctor"
jokes.setup: Sam gives competent medical advice about Larry's rash
jokes.punchline: Larry dismissing it because Sam isn't actually a doctor
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later callback when the diagnosis proves correct

jokes.text: With all due respect, I think I'll go see your dad. Why? 'Cause he's a doctor.
jokes.setup: Sam gives medical advice about Larry's rash
jokes.punchline: Larry's obvious preference for actual medical credentials
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective logic joke with perfect timing

jokes.text: Harold getting fired because Larry asked him to leave his post
jokes.setup: Larry's bathroom privacy request
jokes.punchline: Harold facing employment consequences for Larry's demands
jokes.explanation: Good consequences comedy - Larry's selfishness has real impact on others

jokes.text: Leon's magical vagina theories: "could pull a rabbit out of that motherfucker" and "put this fuckin' Pop-Tart in that fuckin' vagina and toast that shit"
jokes.setup: Leon fascinated by Carl's magical vagina story
jokes.punchline: Leon's increasingly absurd theories about its magical properties
jokes.explanation: Leon's character-perfect escalation of the absurd premise with vivid imagery

jokes.text: That fuckin' vagina could pull a rabbit out of that motherfucker. Or put this fuckin' Pop-Tart in that fuckin' vagina and toast that shit.
jokes.setup: Leon pondering the magical vagina concept
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd magical powers attributed to the vagina
jokes.explanation: Leon's unique voice elevating the magical vagina concept to new absurd heights

jokes.text: "How do you kill yourself when you're playing so well?"
jokes.setup: Learning Carl committed suicide
jokes.punchline: Larry focusing on Carl's golf performance rather than the tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David logic - completely missing the point in character-consistent way

jokes.text: He said, 'I can't take any more disappointment.' That's what he used to say when we would watch the Jet games together.
jokes.setup: Jeff reading Carl's suicide note
jokes.punchline: Connecting suicide note language to Jets frustration
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant connection between sports disappointment and existential despair

jokes.text: "The Jets killed Carl."
jokes.setup: Discussing Carl's suicide note about disappointment
jokes.punchline: Larry blaming a football team for someone's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating sports fandom as literally deadly serious

jokes.text: Leon's condolence text suggestions about the magical vagina
jokes.setup: Larry struggling to write condolence text
jokes.punchline: Leon suggesting increasingly inappropriate references to the magical vagina
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate behavior in grief situation

jokes.text: "I don't want to listen to Louis Armstrong's son play the trumpet."
jokes.setup: Doctor being offended Larry didn't trust his son's diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Larry's analogy about inherited talent not being automatic
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that perfectly captures Larry's logic about expertise

jokes.text: Discovery that Richard's putter is illegal and he never leaves clubs at the club
jokes.setup: Larry learning golf rules about putter angles
jokes.punchline: Realizing Richard has been cheating and hiding evidence
jokes.explanation: Good plot setup for later confrontation, though not immediately funny

jokes.text: He's probably the only one in the club who does that. You don't say. Isn't that interesting?
jokes.setup: Learning Richard takes his clubs home
jokes.punchline: Larry's suspicious reaction to this unusual behavior
jokes.explanation: Building Larry's conspiracy theory about Richard

jokes.text: Restaurant immediately seating Larry alone in the ugly section
jokes.setup: Larry arriving alone to test the restaurant's seating policy
jokes.punchline: Being instantly relegated to the unattractive section without a beautiful companion
jokes.explanation: Good callback confirming Larry's fears about his appearance

jokes.text: "Look where I wound up in my life. In the ugly section. I'm in the ugly section... This is the proof."
jokes.setup: Larry's talking head about being seated in ugly section
jokes.punchline: His dramatic realization about his life and appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's insecurity and dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: "magical vagina is, is now available" / "Normal vagina, I'd wait a year. But magical vagina, six months."
jokes.setup: Discussing appropriate mourning period for widow
jokes.punchline: Adjusting timeline based on the supernatural vagina quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the running gag with dark comedy about grief etiquette

jokes.text: "One night, he just woke up and yelled, 'Watson!'" explained as reference to Jets draft pick
jokes.setup: Widow explaining Carl's Jets obsession
jokes.punchline: Carl yelling a quarterback's name in his sleep like a tormented fan
jokes.explanation: Great detail showing how sports obsession invaded Carl's sleep and marriage

jokes.text: Larry asking widow about coffin handles for his coffee shop at the funeral
jokes.setup: Larry at Carl's funeral trying to be supportive
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to asking about hardware for his business
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David inappropriate behavior - completely tone-deaf but perfectly in character

jokes.text: I really like the handles on the coffin. Do you know where you got those, by any chance?
jokes.setup: Larry at Carl's funeral
jokes.punchline: Asking about coffin hardware for business purposes
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness - business opportunity at funeral

jokes.text: Let me bury my husband first, and then I'll find out the contact info. No, of course. You bury, you mourn, and then you ask.
jokes.setup: Veronica's reasonable response to Larry's coffin handle request
jokes.punchline: Larry's faux-considerate timeline for his inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Larry's attempt at propriety while maintaining inappropriate agenda

jokes.text: "If you need to talk, I'd be happy to get together for coffee, or any meal of your choosing." / "It's a date."
jokes.setup: Larry trying to send supportive text to widow
jokes.punchline: Accidentally escalating to asking her on a date, then her accepting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's social awkwardness leading to inappropriate situation

jokes.text: "If it's fifty-fifty... One good-looking and one ugly, both go to the ugly... Here, a tie goes to the ugly."
jokes.setup: Larry with attractive date still being seated in ugly section
jokes.punchline: His baseball analogy for the restaurant's seating tiebreaker rule
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that perfectly captures the unfairness Larry perceives

jokes.text: In baseball, a tie goes to the runner. Here, a tie goes to the ugly.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining restaurant seating policy with Veronica present
jokes.punchline: Sports analogy applied to beauty-based discrimination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sports analogy that perfectly captures the restaurant's policy

jokes.text: What a horror show. This is a fucking horror show. How the fuck can you do this? It's not fair.
jokes.setup: Larry seated in ugly section despite bringing beautiful date
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete meltdown over seating injustice
jokes.explanation: Larry's volcanic frustration at restaurant discrimination

jokes.text: Larry's turtle death story paralleling Veronica's grieving process
jokes.setup: Trying to relate to widow's grief experience
jokes.punchline: Comparing her dead husband to his childhood pet turtle
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate comparison - well-intentioned but completely tone-deaf

jokes.text: Susie being seated in the good-looking section, Larry's outrage
jokes.setup: Larry discovering Susie in the attractive section
jokes.punchline: His disbelief that she qualifies for the good-looking area
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict showing Larry's continued obsession with the seating policy

jokes.text: "And I'm not a meeskite, you bald fuck!"
jokes.setup: Larry calling Susie a 'meeskite' (ugly person)
jokes.punchline: Susie's profane retaliation with Larry's baldness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie response - matching Larry's cruelty with her own direct insult

jokes.text: Larry making deal with bathroom attendant for good-looking section seating
jokes.setup: Larry needing bathroom privacy again
jokes.punchline: Using his desperation to negotiate restaurant seating privileges
jokes.explanation: Good callback combining two plot threads into a transaction

jokes.text: Carjacker being a Jets fan and bonding with Larry over team's awfulness
jokes.setup: Larry being robbed by armed criminal
jokes.punchline: Finding common ground in shared Jets fandom and misery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd moment - even criminals can bond over Jets misery

jokes.text: Larry asking widow to pay back Carl's golf winnings from the estate
jokes.setup: Romantic moment with grieving widow
jokes.punchline: Larry bringing up money Carl owed him from cheating at golf
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David moment - completely ruining romantic moment with petty financial concerns

jokes.text: Veronica's Jets rant and kicking Larry out for mentioning the team
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning Jets in context of golf dispute
jokes.punchline: Widow's explosive reaction revealing her own Jets trauma
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that the Jets trauma affected the whole family, not just Carl

jokes.text: Larry finally seated in attractive section, celebrating with the beautiful people
jokes.setup: Larry's entire episode-long quest for attractive section seating
jokes.punchline: His triumphant arrival and immediate comfort with his 'kind'
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to Larry's vanity quest, though not the funniest moment

jokes.text: "The only word that comes to mind is, uh... magical."
jokes.setup: Sam describing his examination of Veronica
jokes.punchline: Confirming the magical vagina is indeed magical through medical examination
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that confirms the running gag through 'medical expertise'

jokes.text: Larry having a seizure/heart attack upon hearing magical vagina confirmation
jokes.setup: Learning the magical vagina is medically confirmed
jokes.punchline: Larry's physical breakdown from the overwhelming information
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy climax to the episode's running gag

jokes.text: "Help! Uh, someone's having a seizure in the ugly section."
jokes.setup: Larry having medical emergency after getting into attractive section
jokes.punchline: Bystander still identifying him with the ugly section despite his achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect final joke undermining Larry's victory - he'll always be ugly section in people's minds
